title: Economic Statecraft Is Back. Is America Ready?
author: Madeline Field
summary:
In 432 B.C.E., a year before the outbreak of the Peloponnesian War, the Megarians received an unusual decree from the Athenian assembly: They were prevented from accessing the vast Athenian Empire’s harbors and marketplaces due to their alliance with Sparta, a rising empire that posed a clear threat to Athens’ hegemony. While the official reasoning is lost to history, many scholars believe that Athens, fearful of the rising great power, used sanctions to force Megara’s economic decline.So began the deliberate use of economics as a tool of statecraft.In the millennia since, economics remained an important enabler of state power.
